name: token-dashboard
description: See where Claude Code is burning tokens - turn raw JSONL transcripts into local cost analytics, hotspot views, and session-level usage insight.
readme_summary: Token Dashboard is a local application that analyzes JSONL transcripts generated by Claude Code. It provides insights into token usage, costs, and patterns, helping users understand and optimize their AI interactions without sending any data off their machine.
key_features: Local data processing: No data leaves the user's machine.
primary_use_case: To provide users with local, detailed analytics and cost insights into their Claude Code AI interactions, enabling them to identify and optimize expensive prompts and wasteful usage patterns.
cover_image_prompt: A digital alchemist in a dimly lit, cozy study, meticulously sorting and analyzing glowing streams of 'token' energy flowing from various scrolls labeled 'Claude Code Sessions'. The alchemist uses a magnifying glass to examine specific streams, revealing intricate patterns and cost breakdowns on a nearby antique desk that displays subtle, futuristic UI elements. Small, glowing icons representing tools and files float around the streams, indicating hotspots. The overall mood is one of discovery and optimization, with a warm, inviting color palette. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clear visual storytelling and a touch of magical technology.

name: npxskillui
readme_summary: SkillUI is a command-line interface tool that analyzes websites, local codebases, or Git repositories to extract their complete design systems. It packages this information into a format that AI models like Claude can directly use to generate UI code, enabling rapid UI development based on existing design languages.
key_features: Extracts design systems (colors, typography, spacing, components, animations, screenshots) from URLs, local directories, or Git repositories.
Supports default static analysis and an 'ultra mode' using Playwright for visual and interaction details.
Generates `CLAUDE.md`, `SKILL.md`, and `DESIGN.md` files for AI integration.
Packages extracted design system into a `.skill` ZIP file.
Requires Node.js 18+ and optionally Playwright for ultra mode.
primary_use_case: To reverse-engineer existing design systems from any source (web, local, repo) and make them readily usable by AI code generation tools like Claude for rapid UI development.
cover_image_prompt: A digital alchemist standing at a workbench, holding a glowing magnifying glass over a website screenshot. The magnifying glass is projecting a detailed blueprint of colors, fonts, and layout elements onto a blank canvas. Beside the alchemist, a friendly robot assistant is assembling UI components based on the blueprint. Small, subtle code snippets and data particles float around the scene, indicating the transformation of design into code. The overall mood is one of creative discovery and efficient creation, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The repository name 'SkillUI' is subtly etched on the alchemist's tools. 3D isometric illustration style, vibrant but clean color palette, high detail.

name: kittyfarm
readme_summary: KittyFarm is a macOS application designed to streamline mobile development workflows by allowing developers to run and control multiple iOS Simulators and Android Emulators simultaneously within a single window. It offers features like synchronized touch input, parallel deployment, and proportional device scaling.
key_features: Live display of iOS Simulators and Android Emulators in a single window
Synchronized touch input across multiple devices
Device bezels rendered from official assets
Build & Play functionality for parallel deployment
primary_use_case: Running and managing multiple iOS Simulators and Android Emulators concurrently on a Mac for efficient mobile app testing and development.
cover_image_prompt: A conductor leading a symphony orchestra, where each musician represents a different mobile device (iOS simulator and Android emulator). The conductor's baton, subtly shaped like a mouse cursor, directs synchronized actions across all devices simultaneously, as if playing a single tune. Musical notes, stylized as code snippets and UI elements, flow from the conductor to the devices, indicating parallel deployment and control. The background is a clean, modern studio with subtle digital particles representing data flow. The overall mood is harmonious and efficient, highlighting the ease of managing multiple devices. The art style should be a vibrant 3D isometric illustration with clear, friendly characters and a bright color palette, emphasizing the 'KittyFarm' name integrated into the conductor's podium.

name: allure-notifications
description: jar, that draws piechart from results and sends it with link to build to messenger
readme_summary: Allure Notifications is a Java library that automatically sends test execution results to various messengers. It generates a pie chart from the test statistics and includes a link to the build report.
key_features: Support for multiple messengers (Telegram, Slack, Mattermost, Discord, etc.)
Generates a pie chart visualization of test statistics
Includes a link to the Allure report/build
Can be run locally or from Jenkins
primary_use_case: Automating the distribution of test execution reports to communication platforms.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly robot assistant holding a colorful pie chart representing test results (passed, failed, skipped). The robot is handing the pie chart and a glowing link icon to a messenger app icon (like Telegram or Slack) that is integrated into a futuristic dashboard. The background shows subtle code snippets and data streams, with the 'Allure Notifications' logo subtly placed on the robot's chassis. The overall mood is efficient and informative, rendered in a clean, 3D isometric illustration style with vibrant colors and clear iconography.

name: moss
description: Moss is an embeddable P2P mesh core written in Go and exported through CGO as a C-shared library.
readme_summary: Moss is an embeddable P2P mesh networking core written in Go, exported as a C-shared library for cross-language integration. It provides secure, topic-based pub/sub messaging with tracker-based bootstrapping and NAT traversal capabilities.
key_features: Exported as C-shared library via CGO
FFI surface for C, C++, Python, Rust
primary_use_case: Enabling developers to integrate a secure, decentralized P2P mesh networking capability into applications written in various languages through a C-shared library interface.
cover_image_prompt: A bustling, interconnected network of diverse digital islands, each representing a different application or device. A central, glowing 'Moss' core acts as a lighthouse, emitting secure, encrypted beams of data that connect these islands, forming a resilient mesh. Some islands are partially obscured by digital fog (representing NAT), but the lighthouse's beams find pathways, showing seamless communication. Small, stylized icons of code snippets and network nodes are subtly integrated into the island designs. The overall style is a vibrant, 3D isometric illustration with a clean, futuristic aesthetic, emphasizing connection and security.
